author: The Canons of the Council in Trullo; Often Called The Quinisext Council
book: The Canons of the Council in Trullo.

classification.cautionReason: Conciliar or creedal material received in mainstream Nicene Christian tradition, with denominational differences on some canons.

lineage: The Canons of the Council in Trullo; Often Called The Quinisext Council.
The Canons of the Council in Trullo.

metadata.author: The Canons of the Council in Trullo; Often Called The Quinisext Council

title: Canon XXIV.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
verses.text: No one who is on the priestly catalogue nor any monk is allowed to take part in horse-races or to assist at theatrical representations.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
verses.text: But if any clergyman be called to a marriage, as soon as the games begin let him rise up and go out, for so it is ordered by the doctrine of our fathers.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
verses.text: And if any one shall be convicted of such an offence let him cease therefrom or be deposed.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
verses.text: A clergyman or monk shall be deposed who goes to horse-races, or does not leave nuptials before the players are brought in.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
verses.text: Scarcely ever were these plays exhibited without the introduction of something contrary to honesty and chastity.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
verses.text: As Lupus here notes, the word “obscene” has its derivation from these “scenic” representations.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
verses.text: Rightly therefore has it been forbidden by the sacred canons that the clergy should witness any such plays.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
verses.text: In the second part of this canon by the words “ordered by the doctrine of our fathers,” the Synod understands the doctrine of the fathers of the synod of Laodicea, which in its canon liv. condemned the same abuse.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
verses.text: Compare the canon given in the Corpus Juris Canonici, Gratian’s Decretum, Pars I., Dist.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
